basic_details.job_title: GSSSB Junior Geologist Recruitment 2026 - Apply Online for 43 Posts
basic_details.short_information: Gujarat Subordinate Service Selection Board (GSSSB) has released an official notification for the recruitment of 43 Junior Geologist Posts. Interested and eligible candidates can apply Online through the official GSSSB website. The last date to submit the application form is 24-05-2026. In this article, you’ll find the GSSSB Junior Geologist Posts recruitment details, including eligibility criteria, age limit, salary structure, selection process, application steps, and direct links to the official notification and Online application form.

important_dates.admit_card_date: To be announced on official website

application_fee.details: Note: Examination fee is refundable to candidates who appear in the examination. Fee must be paid online only through Debit Card, Internet Banking, UPI, or Wallets on the OJAS portal by 23/03/2026 (23:59 hrs). Fee paid by other means will not be accepted. Fee once paid will not be refunded if the application is cancelled.

qualification.details: Candidates must have completed 18 years and must not have exceeded 37 years of age as on 20/03/2026. Age relaxation is available as under: | Candidates must possess any of the following qualifications: | Master's Degree in Geology / Applied Geology with 55% or more marks from any university established under a Central or State Act in India, or any institution declared a Deemed University under Section 3 of the UGC Act, 1956, or equivalent qualification recognized by the Government. OR Bachelor's Degree in Geotechnical Engineering with 55% or more marks from any recognised university/institution. Basic Computer Knowledge as prescribed in Gujarat Civil Services Classification, Recruitment (General) Rules, 1967. Adequate knowledge of Gujarati or Hindi or both.

selection_process: The selection for GSSSB Laghu Bhustarshashtri (Junior Geologist) 2026 will be conducted through a single-stage competitive examination in MCQ (Multiple Choice Question) format using CBRT/OMR (Computer Based Response Test / Optical Mark Recognition) method
Part-A: 90 Questions – 90 Marks (Reasoning & Data Interpretation: 30 marks
Comprehension – Gujarati & English: 10 marks) Part-B: 120 Questions – 120 Marks (Technical Subject related to Geology) Total: 210 Questions – 210 Marks

Duration: 3 hours (180 minutes) Negative Marking: 1/4 mark deducted per wrong answer Minimum Qualifying Standard: 40% in both Part-A and Part-B separately (applicable to all categories) Widows and sports candidates get an additional 5% marks added to their competitive exam score.
how_to_apply: Eligible candidates must apply online through the OJAS portal. Follow these steps to apply for GSSSB Laghu Bhustarshashtri (Junior Geologist) Class-3 2026:
Visit the official OJAS website: ojas.gujarat.gov.in
Click on "Online Application" → Select "Apply" → Choose GSSSB.
Find and click on "Advertisement No. 380/202526 – Laghu Bhustarshashtri, Class-3".
Click "Apply Now" and fill in your Personal Details (fields marked with * are mandatory).
Fill in Educational Details accurately as per your original certificates.
Accept the Assurance/Declaration terms and click Save to generate your Application Number.
Upload your Passport-size Photo (5cm x 3.6cm, JPG, max 15KB) and Signature (2.5cm x 7.5cm, JPG, max 15KB). Photo must not be older than 1 year from the last date of application.
Confirm your application by clicking "Confirm Application". Note: No changes can be made after confirmation.
A Confirmation Number will be generated – save it for future reference.
Pay the examination fee online by 23/03/2026 (23:59 hrs) and download the fee receipt.
Print and save a copy of the online application form from "Print Application" section.

Note: One Time Registration (OTR) on the OJAS portal is a helper module only. Completing OTR does not mean an application has been submitted. Candidates must separately apply for the specific advertisement. Applications submitted by post will not be accepted.

faq.question: Q1. What is the total vacancy for GSSSB Junior Geologist 2026?
faq.answer: Gujarat Gaun Seva Pasandagi Mandal (GSSSB) has announced a total of 43 vacancies for the post of Laghu Bhustarshashtri (Junior Geologist), Class-3, under the Gujarat Water Supply and Sewerage Board (GWSSB). The vacancies include posts for General (UR), EWS, SEBC, SC, ST, and Ex-Servicemen categories.

faq.question: Q2. What is the educational qualification for GSSSB Junior Geologist 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates must hold a Master's Degree in Geology or Applied Geology, or a Bachelor's Degree in Geotechnical Engineering with minimum 55% marks from any recognised Central/State university or institution recognised by UGC. Additionally, candidates must have basic computer knowledge and adequate knowledge of Gujarati or Hindi.

faq.question: Q3. What is the age limit for GSSSB Junior Geologist 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates must be between 18 and 37 years of age. Age relaxation of 5 years is available for General Category Women and Reserved Category Men; 10 years for Reserved Category Women. PwD candidates get up to 10 years additional relaxation depending on category.

faq.question: Q4. What is the salary for GSSSB Junior Geologist Class-3?
faq.answer: Selected candidates will receive a fixed pay of ₹49,600/- per month for the first 5 years as per Gujarat Government norms. After satisfactory service, candidates will be regularised in the 7th Pay Commission pay scale of ₹39,900 – ₹1,26,600/- (Level-7), subject to Supreme Court judgment and relevant government orders.

faq.question: Q5. What is the selection process for GSSSB Laghu Bhustarshashtri 2026?
faq.answer: Selection is through a single-stage MCQ-based competitive examination (CBRT/OMR format) consisting of 210 questions for 210 marks to be completed in 3 hours. Part-A covers General Aptitude (90 marks) and Part-B covers Technical Geology subject (120 marks). Negative marking of 1/4 mark applies. Minimum 40% in each part is required to qualify.
